edges.description: Microglial dysfunction from impaired CSF1R signaling drives downstream white-matter neurodegeneration with axonal spheroids and pigmented glia.
edges.target: White Matter Degeneration with Axonal Spheroids

nodes.description: CSF1R encodes the colony-stimulating factor 1 receptor, a receptor tyrosine kinase expressed predominantly on microglia. Disease-causing variants cluster in the tyrosine kinase domain and impair CSF-1-stimulated receptor autophosphorylation, producing partial loss of CSF1R function (haploinsufficiency). Because CSF1R is a crucial mediator of microglial proliferation, survival, and differentiation, impaired signaling causes microglial dysfunction that initiates the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the colony stimulating factor 1 receptor (CSF1R) gene cause hereditary diffuse leukoencephalopathy with spheroids.
evidence.snippet: In vitro, CSF-1 stimulation resulted in rapid autophosphorylation of selected tyrosine residues in the kinase domain of wild-type but not mutant CSF1R, suggesting that HDLS may result from partial loss of CSF1R function.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the colony stimulating factor 1 receptor (CSF1R) gene cause hereditary diffuse leukoencephalopathy with spheroids.
evidence.snippet: As CSF1R is a crucial mediator of microglial proliferation and differentiation in the brain, our findings suggest an important role for microglial dysfunction in HDLS pathogenesis.

nodes.description: As a primary microgliopathy, microglial dysfunction from impaired CSF1R signaling drives white-matter neurodegeneration. Pathology shows primary axonopathy followed by demyelination, with characteristic axonal spheroids and pigmented glia in affected cerebral white matter. In the Csf1r+/- mouse model, microgliosis and microglial transcriptomic alterations indicative of oxidative stress and demyelination are central, with elevated cerebral CSF-2 (GM-CSF) signaling implicated; monoallelic CSF2 deletion rescues most deficits, and elevated CSF2 is confirmed in human ALSP brains.
nodes.id: White Matter Degeneration with Axonal Spheroids

evidence.reference_title: CSF1R-related leukoencephalopathy: A major player in primary microgliopathies.
evidence.snippet: Primary axonopathy followed by demyelination was suggested by pathology.

evidence.reference_title: CSF1R-related leukoencephalopathy: A major player in primary microgliopathies.
evidence.snippet: Given that CSF1R mainly expresses in microglia, CSF1R-related leukoencephalopathy is representative of primary microgliopathies

evidence.reference_title: Microglial Homeostasis Requires Balanced CSF-1/CSF-2 Receptor Signaling.
evidence.snippet: monoallelic deletion of Csf2 rescues most behavioral deficits and histopathological changes in Csf1r+/- mice by preventing microgliosis and eliminating most microglial transcriptomic alterations, including those indicative of oxidative stress and demyelination

evidence.reference_title: Microglial Homeostasis Requires Balanced CSF-1/CSF-2 Receptor Signaling.
evidence.snippet: We also show elevation of Csf2 transcripts and of several CSF-2 downstream targets in the brains of ALSP patients, demonstrating that the mechanisms identified in the mouse model are functional in humans.

evidence.reference_title: Therapeutic potential of human microglia transplantation in a chimeric model of CSF1R-related leukoencephalopathy.
evidence.snippet: we generated a xenotolerant mouse model lacking the fms-intronic regulatory element (FIRE) enhancer within Csf1r, which develops nearly all the hallmark pathologies associated with ALSP
